title: Reading - Part 1
instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.heading: Lunch Queue
questions.text: Students buying hot food should join the queue near the windows today. The usual counter is closed while new lights are fitted. Cold food is still available from the fridge.
questions.question: What has changed today?

options.A: Hot food is being served from another place.
options.B: Students cannot buy any food at lunch.
options.C: The fridge has moved near the windows.

questions.explanation: Hot food has moved to the queue near the windows.

questions.heading: Message from Imogen to Mark
questions.text: Mark, I got your message about the concert. I’d love to go, but I can only come if my cousin doesn’t visit this weekend. I’ll know by Thursday evening.
questions.question: What is Imogen telling Mark?

options.A: She cannot give a definite answer yet.
options.B: She has invited her cousin to the concert.
options.C: She will definitely go on Thursday evening.

questions.explanation: Imogen says she will know by Thursday evening.

questions.heading: Computer Repair Help
questions.text: Students can bring laptops to Room 12 on Monday lunchtime for advice. We can help with slow computers and software problems, but we cannot repair broken screens or keyboards.
questions.question: What help is available?

options.A: Advice for some computer problems only.
options.B: Repairs for all broken laptop parts.
options.C: New keyboards for students to buy.

questions.explanation: The service helps with software problems, not broken screens or keyboards.

questions.heading: From: Clara / Subject: Birthday present
questions.text: I found the book Sam wanted, but it is only available as a hardback. It costs more than we planned. Should we still buy it, or choose something different for his birthday?
questions.question: Why is Clara emailing?

options.A: To ask about changing the present plan.
options.B: To say Sam already has the book.
options.C: To explain why the book is cheaper.

questions.explanation: The book is more expensive, so Clara asks what to do.

questions.heading: Nature Club Walk
questions.text: Nature club will meet at the school gate at 9 a.m. on Saturday. We will walk through muddy fields to look for birds, so wear old shoes and bring a waterproof jacket.
questions.question: What should club members do?

options.A: Dress for wet and dirty conditions.
options.B: Meet inside the school building first.
options.C: Bring birds to the school gate.

questions.explanation: Members should wear old shoes and bring a waterproof jacket.
